strengths: Extensive TypeScript/Node.js fullstack experience across product teams (re:cinq, Optilyz, RIDE Capital), including React, REST/GraphQL APIs, SQL databases and AWS — comfortably covering the core stack requirements
Directly matches multiple nice-to-haves: builds MCP/LLM-agent tooling at re:cinq, has Kubernetes and Docker deployment experience via GISCollective, and used AWS CDK in production pricing systems

Demonstrated passion for clean, testable architecture (TDD, pairing, trunk-based development, testing libraries and workshops) plus mission alignment through years of open-source green-mapping work for NGOs and conservation
concerns: Significantly overqualified for an entry-level position — 15+ years and staff/senior titles raise questions about compensation expectations and long-term retention in a junior/mid role
Based in Lagos, Nigeria while the role requires high on-site presence, implying relocation or logistics to resolve
PostgreSQL and Nest.js are not explicitly evidenced (MongoDB/MySQL and Ember.js feature more prominently in recent work), so some stack-specific ramp-up may be needed

key_factors.factor: TypeScript fullstack proficiency well beyond role requirements
LLM integration, AWS and Kubernetes experience covering nearly all nice-to-haves
Mission alignment and craftsmanship culture fit (green-mapping open source, TDD, knowledge sharing)

response.justification: This candidate exceeds every required skill for the role: years of production TypeScript/Node.js and React work, REST and web architecture depth from high-scale systems, relational database experience, and a documented commitment to clean, testable software. He additionally satisfies nearly all nice-to-haves — active LLM/MCP tooling work, AWS services, Kubernetes — and his long-running open-source geo-data platform for environmental NGOs signals genuine affinity for the renewable-energy mission. The main open questions are seniority fit and on-site logistics rather than capability; on evidence of skill, learning ability and domain enthusiasm, he presents an exceptionally strong case for advancing.
